text: We have been customers for 6 or more years. Don't fall for this company! We got internet a year ago it worked like a dream until about March 1, 2023 now it's a nightmere we have tried everything they have even sent us a new box and we live about two miles from the tower we now have internet that keeps buffering and I work from home! Not anymore I have had to return to an office that has a horrible environment! I am looking for new internet and possible new phone service.

Worst company ever I call 3 times to set my payment date, everything go approved each time and is not. Only recomendable for an enemy. Muy mala. Also it’s expensive and not way you can get into the connectivity program. I should get a lawyer some one it’s interesting in sue this messing business?

I’ve had T-Mobile for over 17 years and their customer service has declined TREMENDOUSLY over the years!!!!! They never had a realistic solution to problems/issues that you’re having with their service and billing. You’re always given the wrong or conflicting information when speaking with representatives. The call center representatives are located in a different country than the United States and aren’t able to comprehend what you’re calling about to provide any kind of support. I don’t know how much longer I’ll be a customer with this company.
Activated a 12 hour test drive on war thunder and my internet has been down for 6 of the 12 hours so far. Wish this was an April fools joke...

Their call centers are all in the Philippines and the representatives are flat-out incompetent. Every time I call they're unable to assist in any meaningful way. Each rep gives a different answer and I have no confidence in any information they provide.

Had a change of responsibility done with my son He took over my two lines back in Dec. Paid my final bill then inFeb recieved a bill for $898 for the two IPhone 13 which was back of the transfer to my sons business account. Called several times and each time the repersentive said they would file an escalation and find out what happened was promised follow up calls never recieved them. Finally called today and heard the same story of apoligy and T-Mobile would take care of this...I asked for a supervisor to call me because I felt this was going nowhere. She called back today and said sorry its been past 90 days I can not fix it for you you have to pay this bill or it gets turned over to collection because it 100 days late very cold and rude I asked for her name Jenn P  306728  please call me at 503-490-2171

Had every intention of switching over to Tmobile from current carrier, but customer service  and the completely ridiculous amount of time it took to get any help at all was overwhelmingly frustrating experience. We finally decided not to make the switch and am so happy that we will be dealing with people who are not just upselling and hyped, hurried and plain out just don't care.

T-Mobile let an unauthorized person purchase a phone without my knowledge, the service is absolutely terrible, and they steal your devices when you trade them in, being on hold with them is almost physically painful. You would think a multi billion dollar company would care more about their customers privacy.

I left AT&T several years ago because the customer service became terrible.  I decided to sign up with Sprint because the monthly cost was much less.  Yes, the service wasn't quite as good at AT&T but for the lower bill I was satisfied.  Enter T Mobile with the merger.  At first I was happy based on the promises made.  After the last over a year I can say I am most likely going back to AT&T.  Now not only is the network terrible but the monthly cost has gone up?  If I am going to pay nearly $400 a month for the 4 phones and watches I use I expect to have reliable service.

I had T-Mobile internet for 2 years on auto pay cancelled because was tired of calling them to fix it. Checked my credit card they was still charging after canceling now they will not refund my money.

Don't fall for the fake coverage map on T-Mobile.  I switched to T-Mobile and got a new phone and was locked into a 2 year contract.  I live in Hampton Roads, a major metro area.  The area show 5g coverage for entire area.  In reality, only about 50% of the area is covered.  Unless you live within 1/2 mile of tower, you will not get a good signal in your house.  I live withing a mile of tower and I get 1 bar.  Called T-Mobile many times over past 2 years and same excuse "we are having issues with the tower in your area, the technicians are working on it.

edits.text:
First of all, the plan price was fantastic.  5-stars for value but everything else is a 0 or 1.  I switched and did the IMEI check with both my phones that were only a few months old before I switched.  Good to go.

I switched from another carrier, got the SIMs in the mail a few days later.  Popped in the SIMs in both Moto phones and both seemed to work.  Started experiencing calls drops, Network errors and horrible download speeds.  Called T-Mobile and they said there were compatibility problems with phone and I should switch to a T-Mobile phone.

Went down to T-Mobile and sales person talked me into getting a LG Velvet 5G.  The model phone in the store worked fantastic with download speeds over 300mbps.  I went out to my car and tried out the LG Velvet and my download speed was 8mbps.  I should have returned the phone then since it is a 24 month contract.  My bad and I regret.  The phone on display is not the same as the phones they sold.  I have to restart the phone several times a day because the phone calling stops working, it can't connect to  T-Mobile data or the phone just freezes.  

T-Mobile got a good deal where LG dumped a bunch of these Velvets because they were discontinued.  About half these Velvet phones are defective and T-Mobile will not take the phone back and I cannot request a repair or replacement from LG because the phone model number is not listed in the request for repair form.  It also tells me the serial number and IMEI is not a valid LG product.

T-Mobile coverage is not what they advertise.  When I got the phone, I was told I had 5G coverage at my house.  I live in Hampton Roads and T-Mobile shows 5G coverage over most of the area.  Not True.  Less than half the area is covered and this is a highly populated area with 2 million people.

text: Tmobile is always bragging about its 5g coverage and speed but I found it much slower and spotty compared to Verizon.

Had great internet service. Like really good for being rural area. 80-120 Mbps consitently sometime 170-240 Mbps. They did work on the two towers closest to me. It went horrible overnight. To 4 Mbps. Said it would take 3-5 weeks and I would see even better internet than before. It is nothing but useless now. Even if I do see 30-50 Mbps the ping is so bad it buffer shows and makes gaming impossible. I was planning to switch our phones over to tmobile because internet was so great, looks like I will be looking into Verizons 5g internet now, because Tmobile seems un interested in fixing the issue. Offered me a 20% discount on a paperweight LOL

Do Not get T-Mobile internet! I have a problem connecting with my devices and instead of trying to help me with the issue they just wanted to cancel out my service. They may be better in the future but they are too new to the internet game and still working out the bugs.

I’d give 0 stars if I could. Bought two iphone 13’s during Christmas of 2021. Got the “buy one get one free” deal. First, the original coupon wasn’t applied to my account and they tried to charge me for both phones. Second, the color they said they had in stock, wasn’t in stock when it came time to ship the second phone. Third, the phone took several days longer than expected JUST TO SHIP. Fourth, the “down payment” for the “free phone” took FOREVER to be returned to me in small installments. Now that I’ve been paying for over a year, my bill has gone up and service quality has gone down. I’m still on the “magenta max” plan which is the highest tier they offer. Service is so bad in most areas that I can’t load or send texts. Most of the time my phone is reading one bar of LTE connection when I’ve been paying almost $300 a month for 50GB of 5G on two phones. Atrocious service. I have several paid services that I use on my phone and the connection is so horrible that I often miss opportunities or simply can’t load the required details. I often miss important phone calls or can’t get ahold of anyone myself. It interferes with my job, my personal hustles, relationships and much more. I will be paying off the phones in full and changing providers and I will not be doing business with T-Mobile in the future. I would recommend anyone considering buying from T-Mobile that they don’t make the same mistake as me and shop around, you’ll find better deals with better service providers that offer better connection.
We’re in Pittsburgh, PA. Horrible cell service and internet service in our house. Many dropped calls. Going back to Verizon. I would not recommend t mobile

I wouldn’t even give them a one!  I have been with T mobile over 15 years and I went to Europe and had absolutely no service and tried to get the issue addressed by adding an international plan as I always do..but that wouldn’t work either, so if I wasn’t traveling with my daughter I wouldn’t have had any service pretty much the whole time..so the only time my phone did work it was without the plan and now my bill is $100 dollars higher and customer service says we’ll we can give you a $20 credit because you used those minutes as if I had any other choice.  Worst experience ever with T Mobile

I had TMobile for my company and family for years.  Around the time of the Sprint merger, their service map seemed to drop off.  I could not make or receive calls anywhere in my neighborhood.  They sent me a device for my residence, but if I was on a call and had to leave my residence, the call would drop.  I would then have to wait until I was several miles away before I could reconnect.  I cancelled service, and changed providers.  I returned all equipment and made my final payment.  I never received any further correspondence from T-Mobile, but had a collections mark added to my credit from them.  So apparently they just make up debt for anyone that cancels their account, then sells it off to collections.  Sounds like fraud to me.  Coupled with their inability to protect their customer data (due to multiple breaches in the past few years), I'm sure there will be an FTC complaint and another class action lawsuit added to the pile of others that have hit T-mobile.  If any attorneys are looking to get in on it, reach out to me.

Worst customer service ever. Their modem didn’t work in my neighborhood, though they said it would. I returned the modem and the SIM cards for 2 phones I was going to switch from AT&T. Dropped them at a local T-Mobile store. The activation charge of $74.90 and initial monthly and equipment charge of $120.00 were supposed to be refunded. Never used the service and their misrepresentation of service availability resulted in a huge waste of time and effort. They did not refund me. Called customer service  (a foreign call center) and was advised they could not refund me because I did not have my PIN number. They could not send me a PIN reset, because my account was closed. They said I had to go back to the store where I dropped the equipment and work through the store for a refund. When I went to the store, a representative told me she couldn’t help, that only the manager could. She said I could call in the morning at 10 when the manager arrived and that she would leave a note for the manager. When I spoke to the manager the next day she told me that I would have to deal with the T-Mobile call center, not her. Then I explained what the employee and told me the previous day and my experience with the call center. She said she said she would call the call center and get back to me. She called me back and said that I would have to come back into the store in person and be present on the call with the call center. A conference call would not work. So I had to go back to the store again to try and get my refund. Ridiculous. I would NEVER use T-Mobile or recommend it to anyone.

Magenta B@@@ S###. Charges u when u make international calls with apps like Google voice and Boss revolution even though you have paid for minutes for international calling within the apps. Other providers don't do that.

Prepaid line with your own phone is a SCAM. 

Went to Boca Raton store 7833 on March 20, 2023 to purchase a prepaid line for my own phone. 

Store employee verified my phone was fine with their own SIM. Purchased the prepaid SIM along with data and call services. When they installed the new SIM in my phone it did not work. They told me I had to wait 2 hours for the service to be activated. False. 

The next day still did not have service. Spent the next morning on the phone with T-Mobiles 800 number to no avail. Finally was told to go back to the store since the SIM was probably defective. Went back to the store. They checked the SIM in their own phones but did not work. After over an hour of them fooling around with the SIM and verifying that my account was active they installed a new SIM. Nothing. They checked the new SIM in their own phones and did not work. I then requested a refund. They said No, prepaid services are not refundable, even though I was not able to use the service at all. That does not seem LEGAL.  After another 2 hours they finally decided to install a THIRD SIM. By this time it was almost 8 PM. This one worked for about 15 minutes. After that no service whatsoever. No signal no data, nothing. It seems that they SCREW PEOPLE on prepaid service with their own phones. CAVEAT EMPTOR. BUYER BEWARE. Seems like they scammed me out of $50 bucks.

First call for new service, guy hangs up on me cause I don't jump on the plan where T-mobile pays the taxes and fees. Wha? Call 800 937-8997 (one number for "everything"). Maybe there is a reason 3 quasi-English speaking reps tell me. Oh, I am sure there is a reason. It's the same reason he didnt call back and coworkers lie for him. The reason customers give 1.7/5 stars. T-mobile is a second rate third world carrier that will screw you if you let them.

Signed up under a promotion which promised a $100 credit card to be issued in 6-8 weeks: that never happened! What's worse, the quality of service quickly diminished. By the middle of our 2nd month we were unable to stream any content for more than 20 minutes before we got that dreaded spinning circle. We made 4 telephoned complaints to T mobil over the next month and all we got were promises. Promises that technicians were working around the clock to fix the problem and assurances that we would get a refund to reflect loss of service. After weeks of waiting for resolution we canceled the service and requested a refund. The supervisor with whom we talked maintained that their records showed that we were getting good performance. Why would we waste our time complaining if all was good? Interestingly, every representative we spoke with during this period acknowledged " an outage in our area!" This company is among the worst I've ever dealt with: please stay away!!!

I purchased 2 S23+ and 2 S23 Ultra.  The Ultra phones were shipped to Tucson.  All good.  Phones great!  Son went to turn 2 S21 phones in for tradein and found a sign on the  Speedway store in Tucson "DO NOT ENTER - UNSAFE TO OCCUPY" and listed another store to go to on Silverbell Road.  The notice was dated December 13, 2022.  Would've been nice to have this information posted on the website to save the Uber money.

edits.text: Customer Service is horrible!!!  That about sums it up.

text:
T mobile is HORRIBLE!!! Garbage coverage sucks!  This is the worst thing Sprint has ever done! I've been with Sprint for 20 years never had this many issues!!!
I'm going to AT&T!!!

Avoid T-Mobile, they are the WORST and most dishonest company I have ever dealt with!!! They have HORRIBLE service; they are unable to do the simplest things  like stop an auto-pay because you decided to pay it with cash in person!! Neither the workers nor managers could do so or acted like they could not as then they paid it on my future months bill which was not even due!! The service goes down constantly and is SLOW, and you CANNOT work from home with it, they lied and said it would be fine when I signed up. They also try to cheat you out of all of your money after you shut the service down, first trying to find something wrong with the tower, then being rude and disrespectful to you as a tag team in the store, then incorrectly reading the account screen and accusing you of missing a payment when the truth is they duplicated the same payment and paid me a month ahead because they are stupid and incompetent, then they finally figure it out after playing dumb for hours and trying to scam you out of your money because one will do business with them because of their horrible service and products and lack of honesty and then they finally agreed to credit the last month of service that I did not use but did pay for, after I let them know that I work for the government and do not appreciate being ripped off and they credited it, which left me with a $20 credit, then I called and asked them to send the credit to me as they had not done so automatically and it had been months and I kept getting the bill with the credit in the mail and knew I would never go back to them, and the first girl said it had to go on the debit card I used to pay my payments and said she sent it there and it would show within three business days, a week later it never showed up, so I called back and the guy took my bank account and routing number and said he set it up to go to my checking account via ACH and it would be there within three business days so I waiting and a week went by and it never showed up, then I filed a complaint with the FCC, the executive office clown then contacted me by phone and left a message and I returned his call and left a message and said I wanted him to discuss it by email as I wanted all in writing, he then waited two days and called me again, so ignored my request, more crappy customer service, then he was a smart mouth and half laughing about my frustration and said "Oh it looks like they over credited your account and you are not due a credit at all so I will just zero it out". THIS is how they run their business and WHY you want to avoid them!!! I have contacted the regulators again; they are committing fraud and have no intention of giving me my money. I am sure they do this to all of their customers so either make them money off them by cheating them by over charging them and not refunding time unused, or by finding false things wrong with the returned equipment or by cheating them and not returning their credits. They are scum, avoid them!!!!! I am a twice widowed 58-year-old mother, how hard up can someone be for a dollar than to rip someone like me off!!! God sees all and they will face their maker one day and regret what they did!!!!

ORDER THE PHONE THEN RETURN IT. TMOBILE IS SAYINT THEY CAN'T FIND THE PHONE IN THE WAREHOUSE AFTER A MONTH. UPS HAVE PRROF OF DELIVERY. NOW I HAVE TO PAY FOR A PHONE I DON'T HAVE $799.99

MAJOR SCAM!!! THEY WONT LET YOU RETURN THE PHONE AT THE STORE SO THEY CAN CLAIM THEY NEVER RECIEVED THE PHONE!!!!!!!!

Somehow my phone had gotten a virus on it as I had been told by a cell phone repair shop.  I was told to remove all pictures, passwords & any thing that I wanted to keep from losing before he did a Factory Reset on it. I did as directed other than saving my contacts which got lost !! I decided to go in to T-Mobile in Lithonia, Ga. and Tyniah who is a Manager there was the one that was able to retrieve every thing that I needed !!!!  She is very good at her job and was very friendly while working on my phone.
Worst service ever. Do NOT join this company. The promise of 5G three years ago still isn’t available

I bought an IPhone XR and paid off since 2019 till today they are unable to unlock the phone. I tried more than 10 times spending 2 hours each time to get my phone unlocked but customer service can't help I went many times in their stores nobody is able to help. Even with t-mobile customer service and Apple together, we were unable to resolve the matter. The bottom ligne is that T-mobile is a scam they are always misleading people, mistreating customers.
I just got a lawyer finally who will follow up and restore my right.

T-Mobile customer service sucks. That's why I left them. They double charged my monthly bill for 8 months and will not refund it, jacked up my bill by $30 on month 3, and when I called trying to purchase an international roaming service they said I didn't have to that it was included in my plan and showed me where it was on the website - when I arrived in said country I had to pay $15 a day for 2 months and paid $800 for minutes on calls (in airport emergencies) that they wouldn't refund. T-Mobile sucks.

Lasted less than a week with tmobile, but have been paying for it for months as we deal with continued invoices! The customer service is terrible and takes very very long time to get answers. Told "I can help" only to be transferred 25min later!

Hi I had a few questions to ask of Tmobile and gave them a 5 star rating! The gentleman that I spoke with is Aliz, he was so professional at helping me with my question! Aliz provided me with best representative service today! Aliz was so sweet and he cared and you can't find that anywhere anymore! Even when I'm having a bad day and need assistance from Tmobile about a promotion or whatever the case may be I can always count on them to put me in a good mood with there cheerfulness and positive attitude that I always experience with Tmobile representatives. Whatever there doing keep it up, I used to ask if they all do a happy dance before there shift starts lol!         Sincerely,
                 Brittany S

This morning, while accompanying my sister to the hospital lab, I discovered that my cell phone was missing upon my return. I promptly visited the T-Mobile store located at 235 N Citrus St, Ste B in West Covina, where I received assistance from Mason McGee. With Mason's guidance, I was able to quickly locate my phone using a map, and discovered that it had been left under a chair in the hospital waiting area. I express my gratitude to Mason for his exceptional help in retrieving my phone.

STAY AWAY!!! was told by a CSR when I asked to speak with a tier 3 rep they did not have one
 financed two phones within a month of each other in 2021 for 24 months yet was told I still have a year to pay on one phone and five months on the other. I only have one line of service currently and Internet through them yet my bill is almost $300 a month. thinking my bill would be lower if I shut my second phone off only to find out it dropped it a whopping $10. As of today I pay 65 for service 55 for Internet 70 for both phones i financed and only one having the $18 insurance plus tax around $9 does NOT equal almost $300 a month should be around 210-215 at the MOST. That $75+ difference A MONTH adds up. 
anytime I asked for them to explain the almost hundred dollar difference in what my bill should be and what it is I get no answers. even the reps are “confused” also when I signed up they had a promotion buy two lines get the third  free 10 months into my service I discovered the third line that I never used was connected to a phone not on my account and was being charged $18 a month for insurance. I know it wasn’t for any of my phones because I was paying $18 times 3 NOT 2 as it should’ve been. they blamed me for not checking my bill monthly when it was on auto pay but I should not have to when I do nothing different my bill should be consistently the same amount. when I took my phones into the T-Mobile location to get them activated the rep helping me took the third Sim that I was not going to use and said she “would get rid of it for me“ so for 10 months I paid to insure a phone that was not mine. $180 down the drain. it also took them almost 8 months to shut my second line off having to call in every single month and get a credit because the phone would not deactivate at the start of my new billing cycle as they promised there’s WAY more but i won’t bore you with a book just STAY AWAY!

The worst phone company I have dealt with in my life. The staff and the managers are not trained with TMobil's policy very well. My information in my TMobil account was shared without my permission. My credit card info, my history log and my activity were shared. The person who went to the edgewood store, lansing in Michigan state  and asked for my info, he paid cash money to the employee who was working there for my information. Be careful with this company, it is not a good company to deal with. Disappointed,,, I requested a court hearing

Been with them for 16 years customer service used to be okay and till days started charging for late fees even though I paid them for last 16 years I've been getting offered from other companies at a third of the price but customer service has not refund on late fees which doesn't make sense cuz it doesn't cost him a dime

I went to T-Mobile to change my plan and I encountered a terrific sales person by the name of Luis Astorga, Store 7532.   Not being a tech savvy person, Luis helped with through every facet of setting up my cell phone with extreme patience and sensitivity.    Luis went above and beyond what I  expected.   Great, great customer service.

I got T mobile home internet yesterday and was told it have at least 33mbps download speed. It had 12 and when I called they said nothing they can do. Total waste of time. I would rather put my cock and balls in a blender than have T mobile.

I wanted to upgrade my iPhone X to a 14 and was told I qualified for the "new iPhone on us" promotion. I added a line to my existing phone to qualify for the promotion while at the T-Mobile store. My billing has been incorrect ever since. I called multiple times to get an explanation and basically was told since I wasn't using the line I did't qualify for the promotion and their "system" cancelled it. I was never told there was a deadline to utilize the "promotional line". I was in absolute certainty that my bill would not change. Obviously it has since I'm writing this review and my bill has gone up. I can't Trust anything T-Mobile is saying. This is the 2nd time I was told I cancelled a promotion (it happened also with my wife's upgrade to an iPhone 13). Promotions seem to magically change or no longer valid. I'm very seriously thinking about going back to Verizon.
Worst connection Phone and internet Both never never go to Tmobile.Don’t waste your money.

Well the question started out what went wrong this time? And that was in a precursor box . What went wrong this time was my wife and I recently moved and she went to pay her bill with my card and got the ZIP code wrong twice which locked my debit card down. That wasn't the problem the problem was T-Mobile hit my card an additional nine times. This in turn had me leaving work early in order to go by my bank to straighten this out. As well our monthly bills have went up this is getting taxing and I'm looking into another service soon

If T-MOBILE HAD A 0 ⭐️ I WOULD THESE MFS WERE CHARGING FOR A IPHONE13 I PURCHASED NEVER USED AND RETURNED AND WHEN I CALLED TO GET MY REIMBURSEMENT MONTHS AGO NOW THEY SAY THEY DONT DO REIMBURSEMENTS JUST CREDIT YEAH THEY KEPT $600 FOR A IPHONE 13 I DONT HAVE AND I AM NO LONGER A CUSTOMER I WILL SEE YOU IN COURT

Still dealing with T-Mobile after not having their service for almost a year now. 
First of all out of 10 phone calls that I had to place 6 of them were either dropped or I was hung up on by the “expert”. Hahaha
The phone calls that actually went till the end and the person took all the information regarding my returned (in accordance  with their own instructions) device, were not processed and therefore ended up in collection 😡! 
I called and called and called - no results as the calls either don’t go through or representative don’t want to deal with it therefore just hangs up on you. Terrible company, terrible workers, no ethics or decency what so ever. All I can do is spread by word of mouth how terrible the company is. They will rip you off and it will not bother them for a moment. This is where government needs to act and revoke their licence to operate or charge billions of dollars in penalties like they do to hospitals, banks and other companies!!!

Where do I even begin? Switched over due to work benefits and Verizon being super greedy without any service improvements…

I am glad that I am not stuck in a contract like others, but imagine someone screwing up so bad that you lose access to some verification OTPs for 3 going on 4 weeks? Then not proceeding with rebates which I just don’t have the energy to pursue and while there was some compensation it was certainly not the best way to do business and I don’t think I would be willing to put up with it if another fiasco like this happens
Terrible service both via phone and in the store

I got pulled into their Jump-On-Demand lease phones by some rep who basically forced me to take the plan even though I wasn't 100% sure. He already had my old phone deactivated and new one activated before I could tell him no... Three years later and two horribly slow phones (One Plus phones good for a year than take a crap) I'm finally here on the day my lease ends, and ready to turn in my phone, and the sales rep at the store I went to tell me there's nothing they can do. I have to drive to a corporate location, which is another 2 hours away. Nobody, in the three years I've been with this terrible carrier, mentioned that I'd have to return my leased phones into a corporate location... I'm beyond livid. This is one more way that T Mobile is trying to scam money out of me. Pay $200/month for two phones, and then have to pay another $135 per phone at the end of the lease to "purchase" the phones and officially own them. A phone that has a 6 hour battery life, and loses signal constantly. I will be contacting my lawyer, and I will spread the word for the rest of my entire life on how horrible of and how greedy of a company that T Mobile is. Do yourself a favor, never go through them... Worst cell phone experience of my entire life!

Customer service representative blatantly lied just to get a sale. Everything she told me was not true. I was out money and no phone. I was specifically told I could bring my phone over to T-Mobile and that T-Mobile would pay off my phone. And that I would receive a new SIM card in the mail in a couple days. And once I receive my new SIM card to call customer service back to have the phones transferred over and that the process was very easy. And was also told that I just needed to take my bill with my current provider to my local T-Mobile store so they could get the information needed in order for T-Mobile to issue payment to payoff my phones. Absolutely everything this woman said was a lie. When I got my SIM cards I called to port my lines over to T-Mobile this process was a complete nightmare. In order to have my lines transferred over my phones had to be paid off and it would takes weeks to do that so here I am with no phone . I returned the equipment and now they will not issue a refund as I was told my a manager. No one knows what there doing at this company. You get different answer’s every time you speak with someone. It’s pathetic.

Worst service ever with them, they won’t take my phone out of SOS mode just because I haven’t given them money after they keep up charging me😡 will never tell anyone to use them as a phone provider! Not even a year having their service and I’ve had bad service. Can’t even leave there service cause they won’t release my phone to me either

I have an extensive background in fraud and have applied numerous times for t mobile’s fraud specialist role. There seems to be an ongoing need to fill the role. However I’ve been rejected a total of 5 times. After looking at the other reviews it seems their talent acquisition team is as bad as their products and services. Get some different people in your talent acquisition dept who can make a well informed decision and just maybe there won’t be an ongoing need to fill this role. Take the job post down as well!!

How do you not have a corporate number??! How do you not have a corporate email?? T-mobile is the worst company ever. They have ppl from over seas who can barely speak English helping you out with customer service! They also will hold your phone hostage after paying thousands of dollars for it just so they can make even more money off you with the service plan! I will never buy another phone from t- mobile and I will suggest that no one else buys their phone from there either!
Slow and independable. .5Gmore like 1G very slow.not for gaming. Took weeks to fix connection problems still run slow customer service was horrible.very unhappy with my service.
Worthless bunch of dumb f*** philippines. Since they merge with sprint they are f****** useless. Waste so much time every week fighting with these pieces of s*** philippines.
Worst customer service imaginable. I’ll never consider using them ever again

WE FIRED T-MOBILE

This might be long, but I'm gonna rant about "T-Mobile". We've been Sprint customers since before they were Sprint (close to 30 years). Always were able to work out issues with them, but since this merger, all I can say is T-Mobile Sucks!

Sporadic signal, (5g yes but strong NO) works in some places but other places doesn't work. Wont roam to an AT&T tower. Almost all calls are routed to the Philippines for support or service. (Cultural, Corporate, and Language barriers make communication difficult at best). 

The straw that broke the camel's back was when voicemail was switched from the Sprint system to T-Mobile's system, evidently, there was an update to the "Rules" of the voicemail that we were supposed to have learned by osmosis I guess, in which T-Mobile goes in and erases voicemails that haven't been listened to and re-saved between 14 and 30 days.

They erased at least 8 voicemails that Stephanie L Rosier had been saving, some for years. The ones that hurt the most were from her mother and were the last way that Steph could hear her mother call her name and hear her say "I love you". 

After no less than 50 phone calls, (routed to the Philippines, except for 1 call to Los Angeles that was transferred to the Philippines) and filing a Better Business Bureau complaint, we were basically offered a hyperlink to some boilerplate information that stated their Voicemail erasure policy and a 'sorry for your luck'. Steph had been saving those voicemails purposely, and had she been aware that they could have just up and disappeared, and that there was an app she could have saved them to she would have. We were grandfathered in the Sprint system and should have been made aware of system changes.

Hence, today we fired T-Mobile and signed up with Verizon for our phone service. In my opinion, T-Mobile should be listed as a very sorry and disreputable company, that cares not about their customers but wants money. 

Please if you can/want to, share this experience we had so that others can know who to avoid.

BTW, two calls to Verizon about setting up our new account went directly to the good ole USA, Virginia Beach and Huntsville AL to be exact. Kudos to them.

edits.text: We have been customers with T-mobile/Sprint, prior being Nextel and other providers in succession for nearly 30 years. We have also been able in the past to reconcile issues with the provider. This is not the case now, as after apx. a week, almost all calls for technical support for the current issue have been routed to call centers in the Philippines. This is an issue due to both cultural, as well as language barriers. There is no option to speak with onshore technical support, nor does there seem to be any method of escalating a support call to a higher tier of support. The issue itself pertains to lost voicemails that my wife had saved on the T-Mobile voicemail system, some for over a year or longer. Primarily the lost voicemails that concern us are ones that she has been saving from her deceased mother. Her mother passed away last year, and while my wife never used a "phone app" to check, store or retrieve her voicemails, she utilized the T-Mobile voicemail system to check, receive, and save voicemails that were important to her. Approximately a week or so ago, she checked her voicemail, deleted a new voicemail and attempted to listen to saved voicemails from her mother but found that they were no longer there. They were the last way that my wife could listen to her mother telling her that she loved her. Repeated calls to T-mobile for support, message retrieval, or resolution have been maddening to say the least. Call center staff not understanding the issue, not able to answer questions regarding the location of the voicemails that were lost on their servers, not able or willing to escalate the issue to higher levels of technical support, or just disconnecting the calls are all of what we have experienced. We've never been told to utilize any other method of voicemail retrieval or retention, never been advised to "save" or lose them, and my wife has always used the save option to save these messages on their servers. WE WANT THEM BACK.

text:
We recently had the pleasure of working with Uriel and Abas from T-Mobile, and we cannot speak highly enough of the outstanding service they provided. They helped us transfer our phones and internet to T-Mobile, guiding us through all the necessary steps with professionalism, respect, and thoroughness.
What really stood out to us was how knowledgeable and efficient they were. They took the time to explain everything in detail and answered all of our questions, making the process of switching over seamless and stress-free. Not only that, but they also helped us save a significant amount of money each month, which we are extremely grateful for.
Overall, my husband and I highly recommend Uriel and Abas and the T-Mobile team to anyone looking to switch over to T-Mobile. Their exceptional service truly made a difference, and we are thrilled with our decision to work with them. Thank you, Uriel and Abas, for your outstanding work!
Kudos to T-Mobile. Love the free access to the internet on Alaska Air with my T-Mobile plan. A truly great benefit.
I have had Sprint service since 2011 and recently had the new t mobile takeover. What a pile of sh it mobile is. Worst service ever
Worst customer service in the world. They hang up the phone in your face. Will definitely switch providers and never use their service.

Purchased a new iphone outright from T-Mobile and when it got to UPS in Secaucus NJ possession suddenly it went missing on Feb. 27th.  After calling support (overseas of course) multiple times I finally cancel the order after the phone hasn't been delivered in 5 days.  They start the process to give me a refund and then stop it the next day.  I've been told that I have to wait 7-10 days to get my money back so that they can confirm the phone is missing, but no one apparently wants to pick up the phone and call UPS they'd rather run through the scripted bs.  Pretty shady to start a refund so the customer will get off the phone and then stop it the next day.

I've now had to get the bank involved to refund me my money and T-Mobile is basically holding my money hostage until they know they're not out a phone insisting the phone maybe delivered even though it's missing and I've cancelled the order.  If you're thinking about buying a phone with them and paying in full I'd advise you to go somewhere else as T-Mobile would rather stoop to a new low of starting a refund to get you off the phone and then stopping it rather then handle it the right way.

Horrible coverage! I can’t wait to finish my contract and go back to AT&T or Verizon. You would think that urban areas at least would be well covered but network constantly slows and calls drop. This has occurred on numerous dates!

My bill has been wrong since December 12, 2021 that’s right over a year and I have to reach out to them each month to fix it, and they keep promising me that it’s fixed and every month it’s not, I tried to leave and they said I would have to pay what left on the phones even though we did the trade in a qualifying phone get one on them…… and worse I said they can even have the phones back and they said I can’t do that either so I’m stuck with them…… and when I told them I’m getting a lawyer that I have all the text chains they locked my mobile app so not I don’t have any of the message’s……. I can’t believe a company can do this and get away with it

Live in Manchester NJ , tried to call someone in my house 3 rooms away , NO SERVICE or signal bars . What if this was an emergency ? This has happened more than once . Will terminate my contract very soon . The excuse I get is that there not enough towers . BS

I was going to switch cell service from Verizon to T-Mobile.  The T-Mobile rep offered a desirable plan, that included a new iPhone and a trade-in credit for my existing iPhone.  The rep said I would get a call from the account verification department within a couple hours.  After never getting a call, I called the next day.  The rep told me that they cancelled the order on their end, because the account verification department tried to call me but I didn’t answer.  I got no such call.  But the rep said not to worry, he had all the original order details right there, and he would simply create a new order for the same plan and new phone.  Magically, the monthly payment went up over $20 more than what I was quoted the day before.  I asked to speak to a supervisor.  After being on hold for 10-15 minutes, the rep got back on the phone and said he spoke to the supervisor who came up with the same monthly billing amount.  For whatever reason, the supervisor chose not to speak to me directly.  Me to the rep: “CANCEL THE ORDER.”  Bye-bye, T-Mobile.

***UPDATE MARCH 19, 2023***

I got a letter from T-Mobile in the mail explaining that they canceled the order because they checked my credit rating, and it was only 222. Well I don’t know whose credit rating they actually looked at, but mine is considerably higher. So now they’ve insulted me on top of lying to me and shifting gears on me with a higher price. What will they think of next?  Perhaps I should report them to somebody.

Don’t get T-Mobile if you work from home

My T-Mobile router started malfunction last week. The Wi-Fi drops every 15-20 min and takes 2-4 minutes to start up again. 

Called customer service Saturday morning who transferred me to tech support. Apparently the model I had (Nokia) had a firmware defect and I was told to go to a store and swap for a sagecom model. 

Called 4 T-Mobile stores before one had the sagecom router I was supposed to get. Store tells me to call customer support and get the receipt for my current router to show at the store for the swap.

Saturday afternoon I spent 2 hours (mostly on hold) getting passed around by 3 customer service representatives before I got one telling me he couldn’t email me the receipt. With him still on the phone I called the store and they told that he absolutely could. Then he himself calls the store and after speaking with them, tells me I don’t need the receipt but that I’m all set to go in and swap my router.

When I show up to the store, the router is right there, but all the store employees don’t have a clue how to authorize the swap (even though the customer support guy on the phone told me it was already authorized). So THEY call customer support and even the support guy they talk to isn’t able to authorize the swap. They ask if they can mail it and I tell them I work from home, so I can’t not have working internet until this sagecom model comes in. They insist that they can’t swap routers with me since no one knows how to authorize the swap, so they offer to expedite ship it, and *guarantee* it’ll be at my house Monday morning by 8am. 

Monday comes and goes and no router is delivered, so I spend another hour on customer support before they tell me it’ll be at my house by Tuesday evening. 

I’m excited when the router actually arrives, until I open the package.

I was supposed to receive a sagecom router. They sent me an identical Nokia mode router, and after setting it up the same issue with the Wi-Fi happens within 15 min 😭.

Back on customer support I go, where this time I’m transferred again to tech support. Since I’ve already done this song and dance, I ask right away if I can just be transferred to a manager. Tech support guy says yes, but after almost 30 min of holding, next guy who picks up is just another tech support guy, not a manager. I ask again to transfer to a manager and this guy has me hold for about 20 min before he hangs up.

I call again and this time beg the customer support guy who answers to not transfer me to tech and to pass me to his superior. He complies and I’m finally able to speak to a manager in customer service. 

We talk, and apparently, not only is the Nokia model router defective, so is the sagecom router 😂. She finally texts a store manager near me on how to authorize a router swap and tells me a different model I should ask for.

Even though I mostly work from home, I’m lucky I was able to work at my girlfriend’s apartment and in my company’s office while I didn’t have functioning internet. Anyone who doesn’t have an option like that and is a T-Mobile customer is in trouble.

TLDR: T-Mobile support and tech teams are extremely undertrained and understaffed, and it doesn’t look like the company cares about improving the situation. T-Mobile is great when you’re buying their services for the first time, but you are SCREWED if anything goes wrong. They certainly don’t want to help you.

Switched from Verizon to T-Mobile and I can't receive phone calls or texts 3 WEEKS LATER. They mixed up our family plan's 3 numbers, forgot to order the phones they sold us for "free". We've been back to the brick and mortar store 5 times. Dude can barely tear himself away from his own phone to help us. Called customer support and they couldn't fix to my satisfaction. Now I'm stuck in a 2-year agreement. Would not recommend T-Mobile to my worst enemy.

T -Mobile bought MetroPCS so all the guidelines did change I don't have a problem with that my problem is T-Mobile is trying to say that I'm using up all my data every month before my bill is due that's a lie because all my device's are hooked up to my Xfinity Internet when I called T-Mobile to tell them about the situation the representative tried to argue with me about the situation because he was shocked when I said all my device's are hooked up to my Xfinity so you're scamming the scam trying to increase my bill for no reason if T-Mobile is having a problem with their cash flow I have every right to be angry don't Target your customer's 👎👎👎👎👎

My Senior Wife and I switched from Verizon and ATT back in 2022 when we both got hit, like everyone else, with massive rate increases.  T Mobile offered us an amazing Cell and 5G Home Internet Plan with our Old Phones.  6 Months later we were so frustrated with the broken T Mobile Cell Towers and awful Cell Coverage in America, Eastern Seaboard, that we cancelled our T Mobile Service.  They ended up OWING us $85.  We have been trying to get our $85 back for over 5 months now.  They make your life a living hell with wasted time, effort, and energy to return your own money.  Talk about Sore Losers.... Never again....

The worst customer service ever !! They extra charge my account 147 dls try to get an explanation but the customer service call center is over seas : India 🇮🇳 very difficult to understand I ask for some else they hang up on me I try again they put me on hold for 1:12 hrs transfer me to 2 different agents and hang up on me finally some one from the USA after the 3 rd call answer the phone and was able to answer my question ,, at the end and after 3 plus hrs of agravación, they still charge me 147 dls because of all the Biden fees that they did not disclose and I have to paid regardless 
I will
Love this company next month they are nothing but a común thefts 😅and I do NOT recommended to any one unless you want to see extras fees on your bill every single month and have to call them to dispute 
The worst phone company 🥹

Having horrible service. Contacted CS and was told that they have 57k people experiencing service issues and that Towers (plural) are down. Told them to apply credit. "We have been advised not to issue any credit". Asked for a supervisor. Not available but I am authorized to provide a 5 dollar credit.  So evidently a superior was contacted but they did not want to speak to me
Pretty sad for work at homes.
Had a service issue previously, lasted days, and was told they needed a that part that was being shipped but they did not know when it would arrive. Told me they would call when it was fixed and arrange a credit. NEVER received the call or credit. 
I think they could start good customer service by being aittle more honest but that begins the ones that are providing info to their agents!
It is no wonder their scores are very low.
I need a credit issued that is in line with the issues they have caused. 
I keep getting notes that they are doing work on towers in the area that provide my service. Seems one upgrade should suffice but there have been 4 or 5 of these notifications. I think they are having service issues in this area (eastern KY) and are having issues getting them operational!

Terrible service. Terrible coverage. Couldn't make calls from inside my house. They blamed it on my metal roof. Yet here I am with Verizon inside my house making calls and texting and using the internet under my metal roof! They take advantage of older people that are not as up to date on technology.

One star is too much for this company.  I bought a home internet, after testing it for three days, I returned it, the quality was very bad and I lost 4 hours for this occupation, I returned it, so far, he sent me a bill more than three times and I called more than 5 times, still the bill  They send me

My wife checking account was hacked on 12/29/22. Someone authorized T-Mobile to take $317.13 out of her checking account, when she isn't on my account. My bills are always paid 5 - 6 days before due date. It does show 2 payments in January on T-Mobile site, but I'm not behind and it also shows I have no credit. They will not refund my wife's account for their mistake. They say it was it was set as an authorized payment. What can she do, to get her money back?

This company fails miserably in the customer service department. I purchased 2 new iphones. My daughter went to the T-Mobile store in Arlington VA. She spent 50 minutes trying to get help transferring her number over from another carrier. Both of the reps working in the store were too busy on social media to be of any help. They kept telling her to go on the T-Mobile App to get a transfer pin. (They had no idea how to get this pin number...they expected her to figure it out, which was totally useless information!) I called customer service twice...finally the second person that came on the line, told us the pin had to come from our original phone carrier. I ordered a phone from T-Mobile a week ago....still waiting for my phone, even though I'm being charged for it. UPS attempted delivery once (without any notice) and then promptly shipped the phone back to TX. The store reps in Los Angeles (where I live) were as unhelpful as they could possibly be. So here I am still waiting....I am as underwhelmed by this company and am wondering if I have made a big mistake transferring my service here!!!!!

Great home internet and very reliable!  Left Xfinity after several issues with reliability and price changes.  Couldn't be happier!  I cut my internet price in half and have faster speeds!  Price is locked in so no worry about paying more a year later.  We recently had an ice storm and Xfinity was down in the area, heard all the complaints over Facebook on Xfinity, mine was running strong!  Glad I made the change, no issues hear and happy to be a cusomer!

i was with sprint,  sprint was great but when t mobile took over they took everything about sprint away, the 1st time that I visited my local T mobile i left unhappy and dissatisfied, T mobiles service sucks and there attitude suck, my family likes them and if it wasn't for them I would leave T mobile and go back to AT&T

I have been on hold with Tmobile for over 35 minutes. The customer service guy couldn't help me so he transferred me to tech. Still on hold. I was with Sprint since 1999. Tmobile does not compare in any way. I just want my daughters phone to work correctly. I have gotten no help but being transferred

I pay for unlimited talk, text and data and for the past 6 months and it has been so slow it is unusable, I live in a city with 2.5 million people so this is just completely ridiculous. I've had 5g for probably 2 years and it has NEVER been faster than my LTE it's half the speed even though I can look out my window right now and see a 5G tower. I've called twice to complain about not having service and both times was told that they were "moving satellites" 2 times months apart I was told this. You could go to literally ANY of service providers and I promise you'll get better service and the customer support isn't given a script to lie to you like some scammer.

I was with Sprint for over 20 years and they were great and then I was turned over to T- Mobile and I  have been having billing problems ever since.  I have been to their store 4 times and  called the company 3 times and still no one can fix my problem. I will be going to another  phone company.2/23/2023

I specifically filled out their online address verification for home internet at my address and they verified I was available for their unlimited plan. I drive all the in town over 30 miles to pick up the device and initiate service and all of a sudden T-Mobile changes their mind and says now I’m not available to receive their unlimited service for $50 per month but, instead want to charge me 4x the rate for 150GB. What a scam and poor customer service. Not to mention they act like your inconveniencing them and roll their eyes at you. I’d stay away from T-Mobile and their lousy service as well as the Pekin IL location.

Really troubled by t-mobile decision to revolution $5 credit when auto paying via credit card.  After recent t-mobile security hacks, I am very, very reluctant to provide t-mobile with my banking info.  T-mobile may be straying too far given their propensity for customer info hacks.

For over a month, I've been trying to transfer my cell phone number from T-Mobile to another carrier. They locked it and refuse to release it.  I have sent a certified letter to T-Mobile's CEO, Mike Sievert, asking him to have someone from his office handle this immediately.  I regret the day I switched to T-Mobile from Verizon.  They've been nothing but a major headache.

I have an activation fee that has not been paid sense October of 2022 and they keep telling me that they paid it I am now having to get my bank statement to prove they have not paid it.  They also said that they would pay $200 per line that we bring over from Verizon they gave me an expired promotion and had to resend 3 months later and 2 months now and still have not received the money.  There service is absolutely the worst I have ever used if you are considering T-Mobil don't you will have the worst experience you have ever had with a phone service.  There customer service is in the Philippines and these people have had very.little training and not one of them could get my service going.  You've been warned.  Don't go with T-Mobile.

Signed up with T-Mobile for 2 phone lines and internet...Upon receiving the sim cards and modem, I was having a difficult time getting it set up, I decided to cancel. I called T-Mobile to make this happen and I had to talk with three different people as they could not understand me. After 1hr wasted, I finally got it cancelled. Well, 20 days later...I not only have not received my refund, they actually charged me an additional $170 for AutoPay as if I never cancelled!? WTH?! Now I get to call AGAIN in hopes to get this rectified.
T mobile are the worst company,please don't ever switch to TMobile, they are the biggest scammers on the world,please they steal you money the worst service terrible connection, biggest liers.

I just switched to T-Mobile a few months ago, I regret every second of the switch.  I aways have trouble with billing, I spend 3-5 hours a month talking to T-Mobile with no results, I signed the contract, but their contrtact is meant to be broken, They are constantly overcharging and the store assiociates make false promises.  No matter what do not switch to T-Mobile, you will regret it.  I call and get many different answers to the same questions.  You can have your crap back because it is not worth the headache every month.  The biggest mistake of my life, and I've pretty much seen it all, or thought I had until the fraud at T-Mobile.

I wanted to switch from at&t to T-Mobile and was told they pay so much for each phone to switch so I paid the activation fees for four lines and when I got my SIM card I found out that wasn’t the case so I told them I wanted refunded my activation fees since they couldn’t switch my service and it took me two months and still only got half of my activation fees refunded. Absolutely ridiculous. So I paid for something i never got and they started sending me bills for service I never had

I took my elderly Mother to T-mobile in Lagrange ga. February 18th to help with her phone. The female employee was rude and insulting and spoke very ugly to ky Mother. We waited for her to finish with her customer for 20 minutes and then she walked past us to help another person who had just entered the store. I have ever seen someone so disrespectful and rude. Especially to a elderly person in a wheelchair. A male employee then ask if ge could help and before I could explain the problem he said there was nothing he could do.

We just switched from T-mobile. We brought our phones to T-mobile and they locked them. We got all but one (ironically the cheapest one) unlocked and switched. We switched because coverage was awful on the phones even though the phones show 5 bars and our home internet device works just fine. The final straw was not being able to receive texts from friends and family but still receiving advertising texts just fine with absolutely no help from customer service other than a credit for the inconvenience. This was after months of data stopping working until we turned the phones off and back on and randomly not being able to send or receive calls intermittently. Until we canceled all phones the customer service people at least sounded nice, they may not have done anything but they pleasantly told you they were working on it and thanked us for being customers. Once the phones were all moved that stopped. I have waited for very long times and not been able to speak to anyone and when I do talk to them they are not pleasant any more.

Just going to quote what I put on their Facebook for starters:

"Hey! So I'm glad that T-Mobile has loads of money to spend on stuff like Super Bowl Commercials, which are undoubtedly very expensive, good for y'all! Then again, who knows how much money has been saved on the corporate side by numerous customers offered rebates for switching from one company to yours with promotions such as having their $400 dollars of re-assignment returned after "three or four payment cycles." 
But then someone learns that such a promotion never existed, yet it isn't the responsibility of the entity as a whole to address these illusory promises. 

Also, I am stoked that y'all are able to simultaneously "upgrade" all of your towers in a certain area (i.e. Phoenix) for THREE MONTHS, without giving new customers any indication that this is going to happen, while giving false promises that the connection speed will be AT LEAST 50 mbps download and 15 mbps upload when a lot of the time its closer to 7 and .08. Even then, in the time frame when someone is waiting for their towers to be upgraded, their personal information is stolen due, almost definitely, to how foolishly so many 'upgrades' were handled simultaneously, making their network very easy to compromise and exploit for information, while blaming the issue, cluelessly, on a matter of "API" exploitation. 

Even then, after the towers are all "updated", I'm glad that y'all can get away with providing, at most, four hours a day of reliable connectivity without your networks leading to embarrassing Microsoft Teams meetings dropping audio, or Zoom sesssions leading to 15 FPS responses (and that's being generous)

Compared to Verizon, everything about your company is utter garbage and there's zero real accountability for how much y'all SUCK. My idea of switching over to save money ONLY because Verizon (who I've been with for ten years) doesn't have solid coverage out here, was a mistake. The majority of your customer service staff is a total joke who don't know how to listen, and even when they CAN listen lie about the reality, because I am guessing T-Mobile teaches them to lie to cover their butts.

T-Mobile, specifically in Phoenix, is a joke. Y'all may have, if it's even true, the largest network, but your network is total trash, and I bet it has ruined the careers of several data scientists and data engineers. 

rot. "

Outside of self-quotation, this company, and for the effects of all things caps-lock THIS COMPANY REFUSES TO ACKNOWLEDGE OR HOLD ITSELF ACCOUNTABLE FOR ANY OF THE FAKE PROMISES IT MAKES. IF YOU ARE TOLD A PROBLEM WILL BE FIXED ON JANUARY 10TH, 2 MONTHS IN ADVANCE, YOU BETTER BELIEVE THAT IT WILL SHIFT TO THE 12TH AND THEN THE 15TH, AND THEN WHEN IT STILL ISN'T RESOLVED A MONTH AFTER THE FACT, THE REPRESENTATIVES ON THE PHONE WILL STATE THAT THE PROMISE OF PROBLEM RESOLUTION ACTUALLY EXTENDED UNTIL THE END OF FEBRUARY. AND IF IT ISN'T FIXED IN FEBRUARY, IT WAS MEANT TO EXTEND TO MARCH. THIS COMPANY WILL NOT STOP AT ANY POINT TO MAKE YOU FEEL AS THOUGH YOU ARE MAKING FALSE CLAIMS, THROUGH THEIR INTERJECTIONS; THEIR PAPER TRAILS DO NOT REVEAL THEIR DISHONESTY; AND JUST EVEN LETTING OTHER CUSTOMERS KNOW HOW AWFUL THEY ARE WILL MAKE YOU LOOK UNPROFESSIONAL OR LIKE A PARANOID SCHIZOPHRENIC. JUST STAY AWAY UNLESS YOU'RE INTO THE IDEA OF ACCEPTING YOUR FATE OF BEING SCREWED OVER BY A TELECOM COMPANY. THEY ARE THE WORST OF THE WORST. VERIZON IS AWWWWWEEEEEESSSSSOME BY COMPARISON. BUT THEIR HOME INTERNET DOESN'T WORK IN EVERY AREA. AT LEAST THEY WILL TELL YOU THAT. 

FOR THE MILLIONTH TIME
T 
MOBILE
SUCKS
SO
BAD

Absolute TRASH!!!! I had no issues with sprint until T-Mobile took over


Customer service is HORRIBLE!!! They constantly mess up my bill and I am over it

And you guys need to stop letting FOREIGNERS HANDLE CUSTOMER SERVICE

T-Mobile is TRASH!!!!

I would like to give a huge shoutout to my local team of customer service reps especially Josh. We are very grateful for the assistance you provided in resolving the 3 month ongoing issues that really had us in a bind. T-mobile remains #1 in customer service because of you brother. We cannot thank you enough!
Been with T-Mobile for 12 years and I would not recommend them the customer service just isn’t there anymore

I've been with T mobile for years and This time I when with a Android S62 cat phone. I had to get the first phone replaced,the gps wouldn't stay connected. Now I have had this second one for less than a year and it shuts  off two or three times a day. If anyone is looking to buy a phone stay away from the Cat phone, nothing but Junk.P.S they will not replace their products unless you have their insurance, you would think as much as these phones cost they would stand by their products.

T mobile allows the stores to commit fraud to boost sales. We've been with t mobile for 16 years and have never had an issue before. We stopped at a store in Texas while on vacation to see about getting a new phone on one line. We were told they had a special and we could get it for free all we had to pay was the taxes. Then they said that my wife could also get a new phone for free with just the taxes. I said as long as it's free we would do it but I didn't want my bill going up and was assured that it wouldn't. Once they started adding the phones the guy went to the back and brought out a watch and said we could also get one for free, we just had to add a watch line for around $11 a month my wife wanted one so I said fine. Fast forward a month and our bill arrived they charged us for the phones and also added 2 watch lines instead of 1. We called and they removed the extra watch line but said they couldn't do anything about the phones because it was past the 15 day buyer remorse. We told them it wasn't buyers remorse it was fraud by the employees at the store and we didn't know until the bill arrived which was outside the 15 day window that they were trying to hide behind. They said all they could do was send us a complaint form and someone higher up would look at it but nothing would probably come of it. I'm definitely leaving t mobile if they don't remove the charges they can have the phones back,  I would have never got them if they would have told me they were charging for them. The store employee knew this that's why he lied and said they would be free less the taxes. This employee should be fired and the store should be responsible for the charges.

The 27th of January I flew from Chiang Mai Thailand, to San Francisco.  The first thing I did was get phone coverage from T Mobile, at 3501 Geary St. BIvd. the next day.
     I was only in town for 2 weeks and told the clerk I will have a short stay.  He set me up with service for a month for $35.  After that, the cost would be $65.  I told them it wasn't necessary as I wouldn't be in the country.  
February  11 or 12 I returned to close my account.  I was told to call 611 for customer service.  They had already billed me for an additional $65, not refundable.
     They are liars, thieves and grifters, charging me, and others, for 2 months when I only needed and used T Mobile for 12 days.  Using them for short service?  You might as well burn your money.  I did get my bank (Chase) to stop payment, and return my deposit.  T Mobile has no honor or soul.  They are greedy, pitiful scum.

T-Mobile customer service has no consideration has you waiting on the phone for a long time I waited 45 minutes never came to the phone when you go online they don't even know what they're doing they have you waiting and it's a waste of time on there too because they have no knowledge at all I would not even call them T-Mobile experts!!!!

T Mobile locked my factory unlocked phone to their network.

I bought a factory unlocked Samsung Galaxy S8 from Best Buy in 2017. Still have the receipt. I used it for 3 years overseas and while in the states. Until 2021 when I bought a prepaid SIM card from T Mobile. The sim tray broke and I had to have it repaired, during which they cracked the back and the screen so I bought a new phone and didn’t use the Samsung Galaxy S8 since then. I recently gave it to my sister only to discover the phone had been locked by T Mobile and I they will not provide any support because they "can't access my T Mobile account."  

I've spent hours trying to get them to unlock my phone; I only used them for a few months in 2021 with a prepaid sim card/number and now the phone is locked to their network and they will not help, give me an explanation of how they could just steal my factory unlocked phone, or unlock it.

Stay far away from T Mobile unless you want to risk having your factory unlocked phone locked to T Mobile.

I still have the original receipt from Best Buy, can provide proof that the phone (serial and IMEI) was an unlocked phone bought at Best Buy and is now locked to T Mobile network.

Still can't even believe this could happen but it did !

Do not get a line with T-Mobile because they are the worst provider. I was supposed to receive a free phone, but that never happened. They kept saying that you would receive a credit in two months. NEVER happened.

I used a Jump lease on my account to get a new phone. It would have been nice for an email, or call telling me my lease was up. Then the line was charged a reactivation fee. Really Tmobile? 
Honestly feel they don't care about us Americans when they merged their company with Sprint. 
No communication or heads up. They also allowed my account to be accessed by an Actor?  Or they just to allow anyone to look you up now, birthday and all personal information. 
What happened to we are secure? 
Tmobile has done this 3 x to me. When will this company respect who keeps them including business? 
I'm furious!!  And whats sad, is that I'm stuck until my phones are paid off. 
Really not happy with their managing my personal information.
